official_functions.role: Name the packet id assigned in Play clientbound state
Decode and write the animate packet body without initialized Entity, Level, or game state
Provide the official fixture action constant
Write and dispatch-decode the full official Play clientbound animate frame

invariants: The official Play clientbound table assigns animate immediately after add_entity.
The official Play clientbound protocol codec dispatches the animate id to ClientboundAnimatePacket.
The official animate body order is entity id VarInt followed by action unsigned byte.
The fixture uses the official STREAM_CODEC decode path with entity id 123 and ClientboundAnimatePacket.SWING_MAIN_HAND, so it does not require initialized Entity, Level, or game state.

The framed bytes, packet table id, body bytes, decoded packet class, decoded field values, action constant, Play clientbound packet table, and remaining decode byte count are generated by the official jar-backed answer artifact.
This contract does not prove entity existence, animation semantics, initialized Entity/Level behavior, spawn readiness, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
test_topology_note: rust_test_target names the current case adapter only; the manifest owns the runnable test topology.
stop_boundary: Do not use this framed dispatch/decode contract to infer entity existence, animation semantics, initialized Entity/Level behavior, spawn readiness, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
